title: acceptEdits does NOT pre-approve .git writes -> the protected path still prompts (ask), it is approvable, not hard-denied

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/.git/hooks/PROBE.txt with the content 'x'. Do it immediately. Use only the Write tool — if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

hypothesis: Protected paths (.git, .config/git, .claude, .vscode, .idea, .husky, .cargo, .devcontainer, .yarn, .mvn) are NEVER auto-approved by acceptEdits (P5-b proves acceptEdits DOES write ordinary nested in-project files). Official permission-modes doc: in every mode except bypassPermissions, protected-path writes are 'Prompted' — i.e. engine=ask, approvable, NOT a hard deny. Headless has no approver so the ask auto-denies (DENIED); SDK canUseTool fires (ASK). Contrast: P5-e = same .git/hooks/PROBE.txt under bypassPermissions -> allow (bypass skips the protected-path prompt). Probe filename neutralized (PROBE.txt, not PWNED) to avoid model self-refusal under a permissive mode (lesson from P4-d).
